probability_expectancy_band_rows.reading: Confirmed board strength plus mature role semantics. This is where the system should stop being timid.
Reliable continuation or admission path, but payoff depth is shallower than the strongest core line.
Higher-variance high-payoff sidecar where upside is real but path quality is noisier.
Useful for participation, but should enter as probe sizing until more evidence arrives.
Noise, unresolved structure, or weak board support. Default is skip and keep capital free.

source_sizing_rows.reading: The board already treats this line as core leadership, so the sizing layer should reflect that conviction instead of leaving it underweight.
Packaging is already a frozen mainline surface. It deserves real size, but not identical weight to the strongest core leader by default.
This line has meaningful upside and should not be reduced to token size, but it still needs stronger de-risk handling than the core leader.
Laser remains eligibility-only. It can participate, but should not steal size from better-proven expressions.
Holding-veto remains an execution action, not a new entry score. It should collapse expression regardless of earlier conviction.
De-risk should trim expression, not erase the line. This is where sidecar realism matters most.

exposure_floor_rows.reading: Once the board is clearly strong and a top-tier line is active, staying below roughly one-third exposure becomes a systematic under-expression problem.
This directly answers the V113W miss rows: on obvious board-strength days, the system should not remain stuck below 0.2 gross exposure.
Probe participation is enough here. The point is not to force size where expectancy has not matured.

risk_cap_rows.reading: Risk should clip runaway exposure, but should not keep the system structurally underinvested on confirmed board strength.
High-conviction leader can be large, but still must respect concentration control.
Packaging earns meaningful size, but remains a step below the pure core leader concentration allowance.
This prevents high-beta expressions from absorbing the whole sizing upgrade by themselves.
De-risk trims to a mild band instead of forcing zero unless a separate veto exists.
Holding-veto remains non-negotiable. Probability and expectancy cannot override a validated hard exit trigger.

interpretation: V1.13X converts the V1.13W under-exposure diagnosis into a first-pass sizing grammar instead of continuing to debug only symbol logic.
The central rule change is sequencing: probability and expectancy decide desired expression first, and risk caps trim from above rather than deciding everything upfront.
This keeps the current control stack intact while addressing the main replay weakness: the system was too often correct in structure but too light in capital deployment.
